data.summary: All 6 criteria met and committed in-scope (typed command/test/judge oracles + advisory scopeHints in schema; native compiler parses typed bullets + warns on bare strings;JSON schema artifact regenerated; npm test 1028 green, npm run schema green), but npm run build fails with one tsc error at the fenced src/adapters/prompt.ts:14 — its list(xs: string[]) helper rejects the criterion-1(string|oracle) union; the root-cause fix is a one-line param widening that is outside the 8-path scope and not in the operator allowDeviations, so I stopped rather than touch it (recommend unfencing prompt.ts orwidening list to (string|AcceptanceItem)[]).

data.summary: Typed acceptance oracles (command/test/judge) + advisory scopeHints added to the schema, native compiler, regenerated JSON schema, and all three acceptance consumers routethrough one shared render helper — plain strings stay valid (backward compat) and warn at native compile; all tests and coverage thresholds green.

data.details:
4 commit(s):
schema/rungraph.schema.json    | 84 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++-
 src/adapters/prompt.ts         |  4 +-
 src/compile/native.ts          | 43 ++++++++++++++++++---
 src/gates/acceptance.ts        |  4 +-
 src/gates/review.ts            |  4 +-
 src/graph/schema.ts            | 36 +++++++++++++++++-
 tests/adapters/prompt.test.ts  | 20 ++++++++++
 tests/compile/native.test.ts   | 33 +++++++++++++++++
 tests/gates/acceptance.test.ts | 14 +++++++
 tests/graph/schema.test.ts     | 58 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++-
 10 files changed, 285 insertions(+), 15 deletions(-)

✓ an acceptance item is either a plain string (compiles as oracle judge with a compile warning) or a typed entry naming oracle command, test, or judge — anything else fails compilation loudly: AcceptanceItemSchema is a union of string|command|test|judge objects; tests prove an unknown oracle ({oracle:'deploy'}) and an empty command throw GraphValidationError. Native compiler emits a console.warn counting plain-string items compiled as judge oracles, and empty typed values (command:/judge: with no body) fail loudly with 'oracle must carry a value' (tested).
✓ every consumer of task.acceptance (worker prompt, acceptance gate, review gate) compiles and renders typed items as text via one shared helper — rendering only, no oracle execution: src/adapters/prompt.ts, src/gates/acceptance.ts, and src/gates/review.ts all switch to renderAcceptanceItem exported from src/graph/schema.ts; no execution logic added anywhere. Tests cover the worker prompt (no [object Object]) and the acceptance gate with a typed command item.
✓ command and test oracles carry the thing to run or the test name; judge entries stay free text: Schema requires command.command (min 1), test.test (min 1), judge.text (free text, min 1); the native compiler trims and stores the body, and renderAcceptanceItem renders '$ <command>', 'test: <name>', and bare judge text (all tested).
✓ Task has an optional scopeHints array of {paths, confidence, reason} that is purely advisory — no gate reads it in this milestone: TaskSchema gains optional scopeHints with required paths, optional confidence bounded [0,1], optional reason; the diff touches no gate to read it. Tests cover presence, absence, partial (paths-only), and out-of-range confidence rejection.
✓ PRD, Spec Kit, and GSD compilers are untouched and their output still validates (their strings become judge oracles via the same compat path): The diff modifies no file under src/compile/ except native.ts; plain strings remain a valid branch of AcceptanceItemSchema, and the backward-compat test proves string acceptance arrays still validate unchanged, so those compilers' output keeps validating.
✓ npm run schema regenerates schema/rungraph.schema.json reflecting the new shapes and the artifact is committed: schema/rungraph.schema.json is in the diff with the anyOf acceptance union (string + three oracle objects) and the scopeHints array shape, matching the zod schema.
✓ a graph written before this change still loads and validates unchanged: Plain-string acceptance remains valid (explicit backward-compat test asserts strings pass through unchanged), scopeHints is optional, version stays literal(1), and no existing field's shape narrowed.

data.summary: Acceptance gate now runs command/test oracles mechanically andfail-closed before any LLM judge dispatch (zero LLM when no judge items), with only-judge tasks warningand the GATE-09 retry meta preserved.
deviations: package-lock.json shows modified in git status butwas not touched by this task — pre-existing worktree state, left unstaged

data.details:
1 commit(s):
src/gates/acceptance.ts        |  86 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++---
 src/gates/run-gates.ts         |   6 ++-
 tests/gates/acceptance.test.ts | 119 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++--
 tests/gates/via-driver.test.ts |  15 ++++++
 4 files changed, 212 insertions(+), 14 deletions(-)

✓ a task whose command oracle exits non-zero fails the acceptance gate before any LLM judge dispatch: acceptanceGate runs command oracles via sh() in a loop that returns a failing GateResult before the only runLlm call; the test 'command oracle exits non-zero → fails before any LLM judge dispatch' uses a judge fake that would fail-closed if consulted (noCall returns unparseable output) and asserts pass=false with no 'unparseable' in details, proving the judge never ran, and the 'passing judge can never override' test proves ordering with a would-pass judge
✓ a task with only deterministic oracles passes or fails with zero LLM calls: when judgeItems is empty the gate returns early before the diff/prompt/runLlm section; tests cover the passing case ('command oracle exits zero → passes with zero LLM calls', 'only deterministic oracles (command + test) → passes') and the failing case ('test oracle fails'), all with the noCall unparseable-judge fake, and the via-driver test asserts no judge slot/pane is ever created for a deterministic-only task
✓ judge items keep today's fail-closed behavior (unparseable verdict fails) and a task with only judge oracles produces a warning in the gate details: the extractJson→null path still returns pass=false with 'judge output unparseable — failing closed' and the unparseable meta (test 'only judge oracles + unparseable verdict → fails closed' asserts details and meta); when passedDet is empty and judge items exist, a WARNING line is prepended to details, asserted by 'only judge oracles → warning in details', and the mixed-oracle test asserts the warning is absent when deterministic oracles are present
✓ gate details name which oracle failed and how (exit code or test name): command failures render 'oracle failed: $ <command> (exit <code>)' plus a stderr/stdout tail, test failures render 'oracle failed: test "<name>" (exit <code>)' (or a fail-closed message naming the test when no testCmd is configured); tests assert /exit 7/, the 'boom' stderr tail, the test name 'greets by name' with /exit/, and the no-testCmd path naming 'greets'

data.summary: Implemented and verified the complete tickmarkr scope clarification, drafting, repair, and write flow.

data.details:
5 commit(s):
src/cli/commands/scope.ts |  29 ++++++
 src/cli/index.ts          |   4 +-
 src/plan/prompt.ts        |  31 ++++++
 src/plan/scope.ts         | 149 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 tests/plan/scope.test.ts  | 244 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 5 files changed, 456 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

✓ an intent file with unanswered blocking questions makes tickmarkr scope print the questions (3 max) and exit non-zero without any LLM call: clarificationGate runs before any adapter probing or routing in scopeIntent and throws listing the unanswered questions (rejecting >3 blocking questions outright); the CLI dispatcher prints errors and exits 1. The test 'refuses unanswered intent without calling the adapter' proves calls === 0 and no spec file written.
✓ with answers present, scope produces a native spec containing REQ-nn requirement ids, each REQ mapped to at least one task and every acceptance item carrying a typed oracle: validateDraft enforces the tickmarkr:spec marker, a non-empty Assumptions section, at least one REQ-\d{2} id in Requirements, a Traceability line mapping every REQ to an existing task id in the compiled graph, and rejects any string-typed (untyped) acceptance item. The happy-path test compiles the written spec and asserts the typed {oracle: "command"} acceptance.
✓ a draft that fails compile is retried at most twice with the compile errors fed back, then scope fails loudly with the last errors — never a silent partial spec: The loop runs attempts 1..3 (initial + 2 repairs); on failure the next prompt embeds the previous draft and the validation/compile error via scopePrompt(intent, {draft, error}); at attempt 3 it throws 'scope draft failed after 2 repair retries' with the last message. Tests verify prompts[1]/prompts[2] carry the fed-back errors, exactly 3 LLM calls occur, and no spec file exists after failure (write happens only after validation passes).
✓ the LLM call goes through the existing adapter/channel machinery honoring config (visibility.llm pane setting included) and the whole flow is testable token-free via the fake adapter: scopeIntent uses probeAll/discoverChannels/route/getAdapter and dispatches through the shared runLlm from src/gates/llm.js; when cfg.visibility.llm === 'pane' it passes a via driver slot (respecting keepPanes), verified by the pane test asserting slot/close calls. All tests run on FakeAdapter with a scripted JSON fixture — zero tokens.
✓ scope never overwrites an existing spec file without --force: An up-front existsSync check throws before any LLM dispatch (test proves calls === 0 and the operator-authored file is untouched), and the final write uses flag 'wx' when not forced so a spec appearing mid-draft fails with EEXIST instead of being clobbered — both paths are tested.

reviewer claude-code:fable (anthropic): approved
- extractDraft prefers the last fenced block over the raw marker check — a valid draft that starts with the tickmarkr:spec marker but contains an inner ``` fence (e.g. an example snippet) gets truncated to that fence's content; it fails validation and burns a repair attempt rather than passing, so it is fail-closed but wastes retries. Consider checking for the marker at the start before the fence fallback.
- validateDraft does not verify REQ ids are sequential (REQ-01, REQ-02, ...) — only that at least one REQ-nn exists; sequentiality is enforced by prompt alone. Acceptable per the literal acceptance wording, noted as a gap vs the prompt contract.
- clarificationGate pairs questions to answers strictly by list index — an answers section with items in a different order or a skipped number silently marks the wrong question answered. Low risk for hand-authored intents but worth a doc note.
- Marker regex uses the m flag, so a draft whose marker appears mid-document (not at the top) still validates, slightly weaker than the prompt's 'start with' requirement.

data.summary: Documented the planning layer in README withintent→scope→spec→run flow, oracle types (command/test/judge), and clarification gate. Updated tickmarkr-loopskill step 1 to offer tickmarkr scope as spec-authoring path.

data.details:
1 commit(s):
.claude/skills/tickmarkr-loop/SKILL.md |  6 +++++-
 README.md                          | 29 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 34 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

✓ README shows the intent-file to spec to run flow with the 3-question gate and oracle types: The diff adds a 'Planning layer: intent → spec → run' section to README.md containing an explicit flow diagram from intent file through tickmarkr scope, clarification gate, native spec, compile, run, to merged tasks; it documents the ≤3 blocking-question clarification gate and enumerates all three oracle types (command, test, judge) with their semantics, plus the untyped-string→judge compat rule.
✓ the tickmarkr-loop skill's step 1 offers tickmarkr scope as the spec-authoring path: The diff rewrites step 1 of .claude/skills/tickmarkr-loop/SKILL.md into two paths, adding 'From intent file: run `tickmarkr scope <intent-file>`' as an explicit spec-authoring option alongside the native default, including the ≤3 clarification questions and a pointer to the README oracle-types section.

data.summary: Added tests/plan/scope-flow.test.ts (4 passing fake-adapter tests) + 3 fixtures covering repair loop, judge-compat warning+execute, mechanical REQ traceability, and the3-question CLI ceiling; build clean, full suite green, no out-of-scope edits.

data.details:
2 commit(s):
tests/e2e/fixtures/handwritten-untyped.spec.md |  20 ++++
 tests/e2e/fixtures/reports.intent.md           |   9 ++
 tests/e2e/fixtures/reports.spec.md             |  23 ++++
 tests/plan/scope-flow.test.ts                  | 159 +++++++++++++++++++++++++
 4 files changed, 211 insertions(+)

✓ a scripted fake-adapter run drafts a spec that compiles on the first retry after an intentionally broken first draft: tests/plan/scope-flow.test.ts test 1 scripts the FakeAdapter with two drafts — the good fixture with its acceptance block stripped (which compileNative rejects), then the good fixture — runs scopeIntent, and asserts attempts === 2, that the compile error text ('acceptance criteria are required') was fed into the second (repair) prompt, that the good draft landed on disk, and that compileNative(result.specFile) succeeds with 1 task. This is exactly the broken-first-draft → first-retry-compiles path, zero tokens.
✓ untyped acceptance strings in a hand-written spec produce the judge-compat warning at compile and still execute: test 2 compiles the new handwritten-untyped.spec.md fixture (plain-string acceptance item), captures console.warn and asserts the warning mentions 'plain string' and 'judge oracle', asserts the string criterion survives in graph.tasks[0].acceptance rather than being dropped, then writes the graph into a temp repo and runs the real runDaemon with a FakeAdapter — asserting T1 lands in done, failed is empty, and the journal records a passing acceptance gate-result. Both halves (warn at compile, still executes) are proven.
✓ a drafted spec's REQ-nn ids each map to at least one task, asserted mechanically: test 3 drafts via scopeIntent with the fake adapter, then re-verifies independently of the drafter's own validation: it extracts task ids (^## (T\d+):) and all unique REQ-\d{2} ids from the drafted spec, isolates the Traceability section body (so a task-title echo of a REQ id can't satisfy the check), and asserts every REQ appears on a Traceability line alongside a real task id. Non-vacuous: it also asserts reqs.length ≥ 1 and taskIds.length ≥ 1.
✓ the 3-question ceiling is enforced — a 4th blocking question is rejected at the CLI boundary: test 4 writes an intent with 4 blocking questions, drives the real CLI dispatcher (dispatch('scope', ...)) with the scope command bound to a fake adapter, and asserts exit code 1, a one-line 'tickmarkr scope:' error matching /at most 3 blocking questions/i, and — via an instrumented headlessCommand counter — that the fake adapter was never invoked (calls === 0), proving rejection happens at the argv→engine boundary before any LLM dispatch.
